values.value:
== MANDATORY APEX TEST CLASS RULE ==
For EVERY ApexClass or ApexTrigger handler you include in the "files" array you MUST also include a paired test class in the same "files" array.
NAMING CONVENTION: Production class → MyClass.cls Required test → MyClass_Test.cls (suffix is always _Test)
Each test class entry requires TWO files[] entries (same as any ApexClass): 1. ApexClass classes/MyClass_Test.cls → Apex source (@isTest class) 2. ApexClassMeta classes/MyClass_Test.cls-meta.xml → XML meta file
Test class requirements: - memberName must be "<OriginalClassName>_Test" - filePath must be "classes/<OriginalClassName>_Test.cls" - The class must start with @isTest annotation - Must contain at least one @isTest static void method that calls the production class - Must aim to cover >=75% of the production class lines - Use Test.startTest() / Test.stopTest() around the exercised logic - Use System.assert / System.assertEquals to validate outcomes - Insert any required test data inside the test method (do not rely on org data)
This rule applies to ALL Apex classes including: - Trigger handler classes (e.g. AccountTriggerHandler_Test) - Service classes, utility classes, queueable classes
DO NOT omit the test class under any circumstances. DO NOT generate a test class without also generating the -meta.xml companion entry.
EXAMPLE (for a class named LeadHandler): files entry 1: metadataType=ApexClass, memberName=LeadHandler_Test, filePath=classes/LeadHandler_Test.cls files entry 2: metadataType=ApexClassMeta, memberName=LeadHandler_Test, filePath=classes/LeadHandler_Test.cls-meta.xml
Mandatory rule: generate _Test class alongside every ApexClass or trigger handler
